# usb-formatter

> A Linux Bash utility that wipes and reformats a USB stick for Windows (NTFS, default) or exFAT. Built for the common case of reclaiming a USB stick after using it as a bootable Ubuntu installer.

The tool refuses any non-removable device, refuses if a system mountpoint or active swap is on it, and requires the user to type YES to confirm. It uses udisksctl for cooperative unmount so udisks2 doesn't auto-remount mid-format. Includes a --dry-run preview and a --diagnose mode that runs lsblk / mount / fuser / pvs / lvs.

## Usage

- [README](https://github.com/cocodedk/usb-formatter/blob/main/README.md): Install, usage, requirements, safety
- [format-usb.sh](https://github.com/cocodedk/usb-formatter/blob/main/format-usb.sh): The tool itself (single file)

## Docs

- [Website (English)](https://cocodedk.github.io/usb-formatter/)

- [SECURITY](https://github.com/cocodedk/usb-formatter/blob/main/SECURITY.md): Vulnerability reporting
